meta.onet_framework: O*NET Skills - Basic Skills (2.A) & Cross-Functional Skills (2.B)
meta.anchor_note: O*NET Level Scale 仅提供 Level 2 / 4 / 6 三个行为锚点，LLM 在最近锚点 ±1 分内内插输出最终分
meta.no_evidence_rule: score=null, confidence=无法判断, evidence=无相关证据

items.definition: Understanding written sentences and paragraphs in work-related documents.

anchors.description: Read step-by-step instructions for completing a form
Understand an email from management describing new personnel policies
Read a scientific journal article describing surgical procedures

items.definition: Giving full attention to what other people are saying, taking time to understand the points being made, asking questions as appropriate, and not interrupting at inappropriate times.

anchors.description: Take a customer's order
Answer inquiries regarding credit references
Serve as a judge in a complex legal disagreement

items.definition: Communicating effectively in writing as appropriate for the needs of the audience.

anchors.description: Write down a guest's order at a restaurant
Write an email to staff outlining new directives
Write a novel for publication

items.definition: Talking to others to convey information effectively.

anchors.description: Greet tourists and explain tourist attractions
Interview applicants to obtain personal and work history
Argue a legal case before the Supreme Court

items.definition: Using mathematics to solve problems.

anchors.description: Count the amount of change to be given to a customer
Calculate the square footage of a new home under construction
Develop a mathematical model to simulate and resolve an engineering problem

items.definition: Using scientific rules and methods to solve problems.

anchors.description: Conduct standard tests to determine soil quality
Conduct product tests to ensure safety standards are met
Analyze aerodynamic systems to determine the practicality of a design

items.definition: Using logic and reasoning to identify the strengths and weaknesses of alternative solutions, conclusions, or approaches to problems.

anchors.description: Determine whether a subordinate has a good excuse for being late
Evaluate customer complaints and determine appropriate responses
Write legal brief challenging a federal law

items.definition: Understanding the implications of new information for both current and future problem-solving and decision-making.

anchors.description: Think about the implications of a news article for job opportunities
Determine the impact of new menu changes on a restaurant's purchasing requirements
Identify the implications of a new scientific theory for product design

items.definition: Selecting and using training/instructional methods and procedures appropriate for the situation when learning or teaching new things.

anchors.description: Learn a different method of completing a task from a coworker
Identify an alternative approach to help trainees who are having difficulties
Apply principles of educational psychology to develop new teaching methods

items.definition: Monitoring/Assessing performance of yourself, other individuals, or organizations to make improvements or take corrective action.

anchors.description: Proofread and correct a letter
Monitor a meeting and revise the agenda to ensure important topics are discussed
Review corporate productivity and develop a plan to increase it

items.definition: Being aware of others' reactions and understanding why they react as they do.

anchors.description: Notice that customers are angry because they have been waiting too long
Be aware of how a coworker's promotion will affect a work group
Counsel patients who are depressed during a crisis period

items.definition: Adjusting actions in relation to others' actions.

anchors.description: Schedule appointments for a medical clinic
Work with others to put a new roof on a house
Direct a project requiring coordination between multiple subcontractors

items.definition: Persuading others to change their minds or behavior.

anchors.description: Solicit donations for a charity
Convince a supervisor to purchase new office equipment
Change the opinion of the jury in a complex legal case

items.definition: Bringing others together and trying to reconcile differences.

anchors.description: Present justification to a manager for altering a work schedule
Contract with a wholesaler to sell items at a given cost
Work as an ambassador in negotiating a new treaty

items.definition: Teaching others how to do something.

anchors.description: Instruct a new employee in the use of a timekeeping system
Instruct a coworker in how to operate a software program
Demonstrate a surgical procedure to interns in a teaching hospital

items.definition: Actively looking for ways to help people.

anchors.description: Ask customers if they would like a cup of coffee
Make flight reservations for customers using an online booking site
Direct relief agency operations in a disaster area

sub_dimensions.scoring_note: 优先评估候选人背景最相关的项目，无相关背景的项标记 applicable=false

items.definition: Identifying complex problems and reviewing related information to develop and evaluate options and implement solutions.

anchors.description: Lay out tools to complete a job
Redesign a floor layout to take advantage of new manufacturing techniques
Develop a plan to provide emergency relief for a major metropolitan area

items.definition: Analyzing needs and product requirements to create a design.

anchors.description: Select a printer for an office
Suggest changes in software to make a system more user friendly
Identify the control system needed for a new process production plant

items.definition: Generating or adapting equipment and technology to serve user needs.

anchors.description: Adjust exercise equipment for use by a customer
Redesign the handle on a hand tool for easier gripping
Create new technology for producing industrial diamonds

items.definition: Determining the kind of tools and equipment needed to do a job.

anchors.description: Select a tool to adjust a loose part on a bicycle
Choose a software application to complete a work assignment
Identify the equipment needed to produce a new product line

items.definition: Installing equipment, machines, wiring, or programs to meet specifications.

anchors.description: Install a new air filter in an air conditioner
Install a speaker system in a car
Install a custom-made production machine for a new product

items.definition: Writing computer programs for various purposes.

anchors.description: Write a program to sort objects in a database
Write a statistical analysis program to analyze demographic data
Write programs to analyze geological data for existence of mineral deposits

items.definition: Watching gauges, dials, or other indicators to make sure a machine is working properly.

anchors.description: Monitor completion times while running a computer program
Monitor machine functions on an automated production line
Monitor feedback control in a processing facility to maintain production flow

items.definition: Controlling operations of equipment or systems.

anchors.description: Navigate the settings on an office printer to print on both sides of the paper
Adjust the speed of assembly line equipment based on the product being assembled
Control aircraft approach and landing at a large airport during a busy period

items.definition: Performing routine maintenance on equipment and determining when and what kind of maintenance is needed.

anchors.description: Add oil to an engine as indicated by a warning light
Clean moving parts in production machinery
Conduct maintenance checks on an aircraft

items.definition: Determining causes of operating errors and deciding what to do about it.

anchors.description: Identify the source of a leak by looking under a machine
Identify the circuit causing an electrical system to fail
Direct the debugging of code for a new operating system

items.definition: Repairing machines or systems using the needed tools.

anchors.description: Tighten a screw to get a door to close properly
Replace a faulty hydraulic valve
Repair structural damage after an earthquake

items.definition: Conducting tests and inspections of products, services, or processes to evaluate quality or performance.

anchors.description: Inspect a draft email for errors (e.g., grammar, punctuation)
Assess new parts for precision and accuracy to specifications
Develop procedures to test a prototype of a new computer system

items.definition: Considering the relative costs and benefits of potential actions to choose the most appropriate one.

anchors.description: Decide how scheduling a break will affect work flow
Evaluate a loan application for degree of risk
Decide whether a manufacturing company should invest in new robotics technology

items.definition: Determining how a system should work and how changes in conditions, operations, and the environment will affect outcomes.

anchors.description: Determine how loss of a team member will affect the completion of a job
Determine how a new piece of equipment will affect production rates
Identify how changes in tax laws affect locations of manufacturing operations

items.definition: Identifying measures or indicators of system performance and the actions needed to improve or correct performance, relative to the goals of the system.

anchors.description: Determine why estimates for the time to complete a task are overly optimistic
Identify the reasons why a client might be unhappy with a product
Evaluate the long-term performance of a new computer system

items.definition: Managing one's own time and the time of others.

anchors.description: Keep a monthly calendar of appointments
Allocate the time of subordinates to projects for the coming week
Allocate the time of scientists to multiple research projects

items.definition: Determining how money will be spent to get the work done, and accounting for these expenditures.

anchors.description: Use available funds to buy office supplies and record the amount of the purchase
Prepare and manage a budget for a short-term project
Develop, monitor, and adjust annual budgets for a large corporation

items.definition: Obtaining and seeing to the appropriate use of equipment, facilities, and materials needed to do certain work.

anchors.description: Reserve a room for a management meeting
Evaluate an annual uniform service contract for delivery drivers
Determine and monitor the computer system needs of a large corporation

items.definition: Motivating, developing, and directing people as they work, identifying the best people for the job.

anchors.description: Encourage a coworker who is having difficulty finishing a project
Direct the activities of a road repair crew with minimal disruption of traffic flow
Manage recruitment and training programs for a high-performance company
